message:
The other day, I was trying to explain to my kids why it’s important to have a pet emergency plan in place. They were completely distracted by our dog, Max, who was busy playing tug-of-war with his favorite toy. I asked them, "What would we do if Max got scared during a storm?" My youngest just giggled and said, "He would just hide under my bed!" 

It was a funny moment, but it reminded me that kids often see things in a simpler way. I realized I could turn this into a teachable moment where we could all brainstorm together on how to keep Max calm and safe. After all, preparing doesn't have to be serious business!  

So, we made a little “Max Plan” on a sticky note, complete with snacks and his favorite blanket. It felt good to involve them and keep it light. 

What’s your favorite way to prepare for emergencies with your family? Any fun ideas that keep it relaxed?
